---
title: "Cook County Parcel Sales"
format: 
  html:
  #  theme: darkly
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    df-print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    tbl-cap-location: margin
    fig-cap-location: top
---

# Data Sources and Background Information

Code from [CCAO](https://ccao-data.github.io/assessr/articles/example-ratio-study.html).

Description of functions and [measurements](https://ccao-data.github.io/assessr/reference/index.html).

Parcel shapefile used in ArcGIS Pro is downloaded from CCAO's Archived 2022 parcel dataset [here.](https://datacatalog.cookcountyil.gov/Property-Taxation/ccgisdata-Parcel-2021/77tz-riq7/about_data)

```{r setup, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(glue)
library(lubridate)
library(fixest)
library(plm)    # panel linear models. interchangable with fixest but have different syntax for models
library(jtools) # for model table formatting
library(skimr)  # for skim() command for descriptive statistics
library(scales)
library(modelsummary)
```

> If property is in SFHA and LOMR, both variables will be coded as 1. I no longer "remove" the SFHA indicator if it is in a LOMR.

```{r}
df_prep <- readRDS("data/processed/df_prep_buildings_TEST.rds")

res_sales <- readRDS("./data/processed/df_allsales.rds")
```

```{r remove-missingvalues}
df_prep <- df_prep |>
    filter(pin10 != "0710101038") |>

  filter(
   # !is.na(high_ff_score) &
           !is.na(eff_date) &
           !is.na(clean_name) #&
           #!is.na(Triad)
         )


res_sales <- res_sales |>
    filter(pin10 != "0710101038") |>

  filter(
    !is.na(high_ff_score) &
           !is.na(eff_date) &
           !is.na(clean_name) &
           !is.na(pre_date) &
           !is.na(Triad)
         )


# remove duplicates

df_prep <- df_prep |> distinct(pin, sale_date, sale_price, .keep_all = TRUE)
```

# Research Question 1

The final data set represents the difference in price appreciation for properties that were mapped into an SFHA compared to the hypothetical price they would have sold at if they had not been mapped into an SFHA.

between sales growth rate for \_\_\_ observations and \_\_\_\_\_ properties.

**Does the value of real estate parcels increase when they are removed from an SFHA?**

H1: A property removed from a SFHA will increase in value more than if it had not beenremoved from an SFHA.

## Model Info

To investigate whether the value of real estate parcels increases when they are removed from a Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA), I used a Fixed Effects on PINs with sales from2010 to 2024.

**For Fixed Effect Models:** The dataset was first prepared by filtering out irrelevant parcels and ensuring that each property had at least two recorded sales. There is an indicator variable for if a PIN was in an SFHA at the time of sale or not.

*For 3 way interaction: In order to include PINs that did not change SFHA status, an additional indicator for post-FIRM update sales was created to interact with SFHA status.*

Additional variables were constructed to measure the timing of sales relative to the date a new Flood Insurance Rate Map (FIRM) took effect, allowing me to distinguish between sales occurring before and after floodplain reclassification. The time between sales was measured in both months and years.

These models tested whether being removed from the SFHA is associated with a statistically significant increase in log sale price.

I also explored interaction terms with LOMR status. Owners can appeal their flood zone designation and have the map altered. This results in them no longer having mandatory flood insurance if their appeal is accepted. I also included alternative specifications, such as including binary indicators specifically for whether a parcel was added or removed from an SFHA.

All models clustered standard errors at the parcel (PIN) level to account for repeated observations of the same property.

-   Robustness checks: cluster at eff_date or muni level, vcov = "hetero", etc.

Notes:

1.  Removal from the SFHA is due to the FEMA flood insurance rate maps being updated. Properties are either in or out of the "1-in-100" year flood plain. They can be located in the SFHA and also have a LOMR that "removes" them from the floodplain. This information is represented in multiple variables (`in_eff_sfha` and `in_lomr`). If an observation is coded as `Removed` from an SFHA, it indicates that the floodplain shapefile itself changed that was created by FEMA.

-   If a property was in an SFHA in a FIRM and had an approved LOMR and then the FIRM is updated years later and the LOMR was still viewed as accurate and valid, then the PIN will appear as "removed from the SFHA" because the updated FIRM will incorporate the old LOMR into the updated floodplain.

2.  Treatment window timing: In theory, EFF_DATE would be associated with a change in price due to the capitalization of mandatory flood insurance requirement for homes that have mortgages in floodplains. The knowledge of the risk and the potential loss due to future floods may decrease the amount that buyers are willing to pay for a property that they know see as more risky even if the financial ties to required flood insurance are not effective yet. PRE_DATE is used due to a buyers anticipation of a policy's impact.

-   There are properties that seem to be added or removed from SFHAs in years where they are not updated.

Concerns:

-   Who's included or excluded? Are the properties that are in floodplains different in ways that impact the sales? Are people less willing to buy risky properties in general, resulting in fewer sales?

-   Inflation? Market trends? Year or regional fixed effects?

-   If parcels were removed from the FEMA flood zone but still at risk, does that say more about information asymmetry or regulatory trust than flood safety?

-   Areas that have received new flood maps are mostly wealthier parts of Cook County. The 800+ pins added to flood plains in the preliminary FIRM are all in Northern Cook County. The FIRMs updated in 2018 and 2021 are along the lake front and include Glencoe/Wilmette areas. Could really influence price trends.

Contributions to the literature: Many models model if SFHA status changes, but have it as one variable. this assumes that the effect of being added in or removed from an SFHA is the same in both directions. I model in_eff_sfha as one variable and separate it into added and removed to see if there are different effects.

```{r}
#skim(df_prep)
```

## Variable Coding

> Note: there are PINs located in LOMRs that are NOT located in SFHAs (like in Wheeling, where nearly the entire area is in one large LOMR.) Because I am looking at the removal of the flood insurance requirement, I only code properties as being in a lomr if they are also in an SFHA. 


> Check relative year is calculated. Can it be +.5 and-.5 instead of 0?

```{r recode-dfpreptest}
table(df_prep$addedto_eff_sfha)

df_prep <- df_prep |> 
  # pin didn't exist in 2018 but did in 2024, so got considered as mapped out?
  mutate(
    removedfrom_eff_sfha = ifelse(pin10 == "1729309054", FALSE, removedfrom_eff_sfha),
    
    in_eff_sfha = ifelse(is.na(in_eff_sfha), FALSE, in_eff_sfha),
    in_prelim_sfha = ifelse(is.na(in_prelim_sfha), FALSE, in_prelim_sfha),
    
    addedto_eff_sfha = ifelse(is.na(addedto_eff_sfha), FALSE, addedto_eff_sfha),
    removedfrom_eff_sfha = ifelse(is.na(removedfrom_eff_sfha), FALSE, removedfrom_eff_sfha), 
    addedto_prelim_sfha = ifelse(is.na(addedto_prelim_sfha), FALSE, addedto_prelim_sfha),
    removedfrom_prelim_sfha = ifelse(is.na(removedfrom_prelim_sfha), FALSE, removedfrom_prelim_sfha)

         ) 

df_prep <- df_prep |>
  
  mutate(
    # so I can subset by FIRM date in later steps
    eff_date_chr = as.factor(eff_date),
    pre_date_chr = as.factor(pre_date),
    
    added_in    = addedto_eff_sfha == TRUE,
    removed_out = removedfrom_eff_sfha == TRUE & in_lomr != "Received LOMR",
    added_in_prelim    = addedto_prelim_sfha == TRUE,
    removed_out_prelim = removedfrom_prelim_sfha == TRUE & in_lomr != "Received LOMR"
  ) |>
  group_by(pin) |>
  mutate(
    # first year it was mapped IN (per PIN)
    treat_year_add_eff = ifelse((any(added_in == TRUE) ), 
                                # & eff_date_chr != "2008-08-19", 
                                year(eff_date), 10000),  # never treated set to 10,000
    
    # first year it was mapped OUT (per PIN)
    treat_year_remove_eff = ifelse(any(removed_out== TRUE)
                                   & eff_date_chr != "2008-08-19", 
                                   year(eff_date), 10000),
    
    
    # Added ore removed from Preliminary FIRMs
    treat_year_add_prelim = ifelse((any(added_in_prelim == TRUE) ), year(pre_date), 10000),  # never treated set to 10,000
    
    # first year it was mapped OUT (per PIN)
    treat_year_remove_prelim = ifelse(any(removed_out_prelim== TRUE), year(pre_date), 10000)
    
    
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    
    # Event time (relative year) for each outcome
    # coded to match S&A's method of relative time
    
    rel_year_add_eff    = ifelse(treat_year_add_eff != 10000,    sale_year - treat_year_add_eff,    -1000),
    rel_year_remove_eff = ifelse(treat_year_remove_eff != 10000, sale_year - treat_year_remove_eff, -1000),
    
    
    rel_year_add_prelim    = ifelse(treat_year_add_prelim != 10000,    sale_year - treat_year_add_prelim,    -1000),
    rel_year_add_prelim = ifelse(rel_year_add_prelim == 0 & sale_date < pre_date, -1, rel_year_add_prelim),
    rel_year_remove_prelim = ifelse(treat_year_remove_prelim != 10000, sale_year - treat_year_remove_prelim, -1000),
    rel_year_remove_prelim = ifelse(rel_year_remove_prelim ==0 & sale_date < pre_date, -1, rel_year_remove_prelim)
    
  ) |>
  mutate(
    qtr  = quarter(sale_date),     # 1..4
    year = year(sale_date),            
    sale_qtr_dec = year + (qtr - 1) / 4,        # 2012.00, .25, .50, .75
    
    group_name = case_when(  # group variable, based on the time period that FIRMs were updated. which is when they were first treated.
      # used for callaway way I think? Delete soon or move. not needed for S&A method
      eff_date == "2008-08-19" ~ 0,
      eff_date == "2019-11-01" ~ 2019,
      eff_date == "2021-09-10" ~ 2021,
      TRUE ~ NA),
    
    # delete once checked that code still works if commented out
   # rel_time = round(lubridate::as.difftime(sale_date - eff_date) / 365), # get it to approximate years from event
  #  time_to_treat = round((sale_date - eff_date)/365)
    )
```


```{r recode-df_prep}
#| eval: false

table(df_prep$addedto_eff_sfha)

df_prep <- df_prep |> 
  # pin didn't exist in 2018 but did in 2024, so got considered as mapped out?
  mutate(
    removedfrom_eff_sfha = ifelse(pin10 == "1729309054", "0", removedfrom_eff_sfha),
         )




df_prep$removedfrom_eff_sfha
  
df_prep <- df_prep |>
  
  mutate(
    # so I can subset by FIRM date in later steps
    eff_date_chr = as.factor(eff_date),
    pre_date_chr = as.factor(pre_date),
    
    added_in    = addedto_eff_sfha == "MappedIn",
    removed_out = removedfrom_eff_sfha == "MappedOut" & in_lomr != "Received LOMR",
    added_in_prelim    = addedto_prelim_sfha == "MappedIn",
    removed_out_prelim = removedfrom_prelim_sfha == "MappedOut" & in_lomr != "Received LOMR"
  ) |>
  group_by(pin) |>
  mutate(
    # first year it was mapped IN (per PIN)
    treat_year_add_eff = ifelse((any(added_in == TRUE) ), 
                                # & eff_date_chr != "2008-08-19", 
                                year(eff_date), 10000),  # never treated set to 10,000
    
    # first year it was mapped OUT (per PIN)
    treat_year_remove_eff = ifelse(any(removed_out== TRUE)
                                   & eff_date_chr != "2008-08-19", 
                                   year(eff_date), 10000),
    
    
    # Added ore removed from Preliminary FIRMs
    treat_year_add_prelim = ifelse((any(added_in_prelim == TRUE) ), year(pre_date), 10000),  # never treated set to 10,000
    
    # first year it was mapped OUT (per PIN)
    treat_year_remove_prelim = ifelse(any(removed_out_prelim== TRUE), year(pre_date), 10000)
    
    
  ) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    
    # Event time (relative year) for each outcome
    # coded to match S&A's method of relative time
    
    rel_year_add_eff    = ifelse(treat_year_add_eff != 10000,    sale_year - treat_year_add_eff,    -1000),
    rel_year_remove_eff = ifelse(treat_year_remove_eff != 10000, sale_year - treat_year_remove_eff, -1000),
    
    
    rel_year_add_prelim    = ifelse(treat_year_add_prelim != 10000,    sale_year - treat_year_add_prelim,    -1000),
    rel_year_add_prelim = ifelse(rel_year_add_prelim == 0 & sale_date < pre_date, -1, rel_year_add_prelim),
    rel_year_remove_prelim = ifelse(treat_year_remove_prelim != 10000, sale_year - treat_year_remove_prelim, -1000),
    rel_year_remove_prelim = ifelse(rel_year_remove_prelim ==0 & sale_date < pre_date, -1, rel_year_remove_prelim)
    
  ) |>
  mutate(
    qtr  = quarter(sale_date),     # 1..4
    year = year(sale_date),            
    sale_qtr_dec = year + (qtr - 1) / 4,        # 2012.00, .25, .50, .75
    
    group_name = case_when(  # group variable, based on the time period that FIRMs were updated. which is when they were first treated.
      # used for callaway way I think? Delete soon or move. not needed for S&A method
      eff_date == "2008-08-19" ~ 0,
      eff_date == "2019-11-01" ~ 2019,
      eff_date == "2021-09-10" ~ 2021,
      TRUE ~ NA),
    
    # delete once checked that code still works if commented out
   # rel_time = round(lubridate::as.difftime(sale_date - eff_date) / 365), # get it to approximate years from event
  #  time_to_treat = round((sale_date - eff_date)/365)
    )
```

```{r}
#| layout-ncol: 2

table(df_prep$sale_year, df_prep$treat_year_add_eff)

table(df_prep$sale_year, df_prep$treat_year_remove_eff)


table(df_prep$sale_year[df_prep$sale_year>=2016], df_prep$addedto_eff_sfha[df_prep$sale_year>=2016])

table(df_prep$sale_year[df_prep$sale_year>=2016], df_prep$removedfrom_eff_sfha[df_prep$sale_year>=2016])
table(df_prep$sale_year[df_prep$sale_year>=2016], df_prep$removedfrom_eff_sfha[df_prep$sale_year>=2016], df_prep$in_lomr[df_prep$sale_year>=2016])

```

```{r}
#| eval: false

# groups by PIN and year and calculates the average sale price (PLM does not like duplicate observations per id-time unit in its panel regressions).

df_prep |> group_by(pin, sale_year) |> 
  mutate(n = n()) |>
  filter(n() > 1) |>
  select(pin, n, sale_year, flood_factor_score, sale_price) |> arrange(desc(n))

df_prep |> group_by(pin, sale_year) |> filter(n() > 1) |> ungroup() |> distinct(pin)

df_prep |> group_by(pin, sale_year) |>  
  filter(n() > 1) |> # keep only PINs that sold multiple times within a year
  mutate(parcel = str_sub(pin, 1, 10) ) |>
  mutate(pins_in_pacel = n_distinct(pin)) |>
  arrange(desc(pins_in_pacel)) |>
  filter(n() > 1) |> ungroup() |> distinct(parcel)

```

```{r}
table(df_prep$eff_date_chr)
table(df_prep$pre_date_chr)

table(df_prep$addedto_eff_sfha)
table(df_prep$removedfrom_eff_sfha)

table(df_prep$addedto_prelim_sfha)
table(df_prep$removedfrom_prelim_sfha)

table(df_prep$treat_year_remove_eff)
table(df_prep$treat_year_remove_prelim)

table(df_prep$rel_year_remove_eff)
table(df_prep$rel_year_remove_prelim)

```


# Models

## FIRM updates as Information Events

>  move models from below here. FIRM Updates are the treatment (preliminary and effective: do both)

### County Wide Models

Potentially wrong estimates due to staggered treatment of FIRM updates.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-feolsmodels-fixedeffects

effective_twfe <- list(
  
  "Eff SFHA" = feols(log(sale_price) ~ in_eff_sfha  | pin + sale_year,  
                     cluster = ~ pin + clean_name,
                     
                     data = df_prep
  ),
    "Eff SFHA" = feols(log(sale_price) ~ in_eff_sfha  | pin + sale_year,  
                     cluster = ~ clean_name,
                     
                     data = df_prep
  ),
  "Eff - Add or Remove" = feols(log(sale_price) ~ addedto_eff_sfha + removedfrom_eff_sfha  | pin + sale_year,  
                            cluster = ~ pin + clean_name,
                            
                            data = df_prep
  ) ,
  "Eff - Add or Remove" = feols(log(sale_price) ~ addedto_eff_sfha + removedfrom_eff_sfha  | pin + sale_year,  
                            cluster =  ~ clean_name,
                            
                            data = df_prep
  ),
  "Eff - Add or Remove" = feols(log(sale_price) ~ addedto_eff_sfha*Triad + removedfrom_eff_sfha*Triad  | pin + sale_year,  
                            cluster =  ~ clean_name,
                            
                            data = df_prep
  )
)

prelim_twfe <- list(  
  
  "Prelim SFHA" = feols(log(sale_price) ~ in_prelim_sfha | pin + sale_year,
                        cluster =  ~ pin + clean_name,
                        data = df_prep
  ),
    "Prelim SFHA" = feols(log(sale_price) ~ in_prelim_sfha*Triad | pin + sale_year,
                        cluster =  ~ pin + clean_name,
                        data = df_prep
  ),
  
  "Prelim - Add or Remove" = feols(log(sale_price) ~ addedto_prelim_sfha + removedfrom_prelim_sfha | pin + sale_year,
                                cluster=  ~ pin + clean_name,
                                data = df_prep
                                
  ),
  
  "Prelim - Add or Remove" = feols(log(sale_price) ~ addedto_prelim_sfha*Triad + removedfrom_prelim_sfha*Triad | pin + sale_year,
                                cluster=  ~ pin + clean_name,
                                data = df_prep
                                
  )
) 


all_models_formatted <- modelsummary::modelsummary(effective_twfe,
               output = "flextable",
               fmt = function(x) round(x, 2),

             stars = TRUE)

all_models_formatted
```



```{r}
all_models_formatted <- modelsummary::modelsummary(prelim_twfe,
               output = "flextable",
               fmt = function(x) round(x, 2),

             stars = TRUE)

all_models_formatted
```




### Subsets for policy rollouts


#### Effective FIRMs



```{r}
#| column: page

feols(log(sale_price) ~ sw(added_in) | pin + sale_year,
      data = df_prep,
     subset = ~eff_date_chr != "2008-08-19",
      cluster = ~pin,
      fsplit= ~eff_date_chr
) |> modelsummary(stars = TRUE)
```



#### Preliminary FIRMs

```{r}
#| column: page

df_prep |> #filter(year(pre_date) > 2014) |>
  feols(log(sale_price) ~ sw(in_prelim_sfha, added_in_prelim, removed_out_prelim) | pin + sale_year,
        fsplit= ~pre_date,
        cluster = ~pin
        
  ) |> modelsummary(stars = TRUE)

```


```{r}
#| eval: false

table(df_prep$in_prelim_sfha)

df_prep <- df_prep |>
  ungroup() |>
  group_by(pin) |>
  arrange(sale_date) |>
  mutate(
    addedto_prelim = if_else(
      (in_prelim_sfha == "SFHA" & dplyr::lag(in_prelim_sfha, 1) == "Not SFHA" ), "Added", "0"),
         
        # removedfrom_prelim = if_else(
       #    (in_prelim_sfha == "Not SFHA" & dplyr::lag(in_prelim_sfha,1) == "SFHA") , "Removed", "0")
    ) |>
  ungroup()
  

table(df_prep$addedto_prelim)
```

```{r}
#| eval: false


m1 <- feols(log(sale_price) ~ addedto_prelim | pin + sale_year, data = df_prep |> filter(pre_date_chr=="2015-02-12"))


m3 <- feols(log(sale_price) ~ addedto_prelim | pin + sale_year, data = df_prep |> filter(pre_date_chr == "2019-06-26"))


m5 <- feols(log(sale_price) ~ addedto_prelim | pin + sale_year, data = df_prep |> filter(pre_date_chr == "2021-09-22"))

models <- list("Prelim 1" = m1, "Prelim 2" = m3, "Prelim 3" = m5)

# --- Define clustering schemes (edit to your geography variables) ---
clusters <- list(
  "PIN"         = ~ pin,
  "PIN × Year"  = ~ pin + sale_year,
  "Muni × Year" = ~ clean_name + sale_year  # or municipality if that's your column
)

# --- Build summaries for all model × cluster combos ---
sums <- list()
for (mname in names(models)) {
  for (cname in names(clusters)) {
    lbl <- paste(mname, "·", cname)  # e.g., "M1 · PIN"
    sums[[lbl]] <- summary(models[[mname]], cluster = clusters[[cname]])
  }
}


library(modelsummary)
# --- Make nested-column table (panels group by model) ---
ms_tbl <- modelsummary(
  sums,
#  output = "html",              # "html", "latex", "docx" also work
  stars = TRUE,
  gof_omit = "IC|Log|Adj|Pseudo",  # keep the table tight
  escape = FALSE                   # keep the × symbol
)

ms_tbl

```







## Staggered Policy Rollout Methods

```{r}
#| label: fig-feolscomparison-removedfromfirms
#| fig-cap: "TWFE to compare with sunab() output. Uses Effective FIRM dates and year relative to treatment year."

eff_noLOMRPINs = feols(log(sale_price) ~ i(rel_year_remove_eff,  ref =c(-1, -1000 ))  | 
                         pin + sale_year,   
                       cluster = ~clean_name,
                       data = df_prep)
#summary(eff_noLOMRPINs)
#iplot(eff_noLOMRPINs)

est_sa_nolomrs = feols(log(sale_price) ~ sunab(treat_year_remove_eff, rel_year_remove_eff) | 
                         pin + sale_year, 
                       cluster = ~clean_name,
                       data = df_prep)

iplot(list(eff_noLOMRPINs, est_sa_nolomrs), main = "Removed from Eff SFHAs. TWFE & S&A20")

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-feolscomparison-removedfromfirms-nolomrs
#| fig-cap: "TWFE to compare with sunab() output. Uses Effective FIRM dates and year relative to treatment year. Excludes PINs that had LOMRs!"

eff_noLOMRPINs = feols(log(sale_price) ~ i(rel_year_remove_eff,  ref =c(-1, -1000 ))  | 
                         pin + sale_year,   
                       cluster = ~clean_name,
                       subset = ~in_lomr == "Not in LOMR" & !is.na(clean_name),
                       data = df_prep)
#summary(eff_noLOMRPINs)
#iplot(eff_noLOMRPINs)

est_sa_nolomrs = feols(log(sale_price) ~ sunab(treat_year_remove_eff, rel_year_remove_eff) | 
                         pin + sale_year, 
                       cluster = ~clean_name,
                       subset = ~in_lomr == "Not in LOMR" & !is.na(clean_name),
                       data = df_prep)

iplot(list(eff_noLOMRPINs, est_sa_nolomrs), main = "Removed from Eff SFHAs. Excludes LOMR PINs")

```




```{r}
#| label: tbl-feolscomparison-addedtofirms-nolomrs
#| tbl-cap: "Effect of being added to  firms in preliminary and effective FIRM updates.  Excludes PINs that had LOMRs!"
#| layout-ncol: 2
#| fig-cap: "Added to SFHA - Excludes PINs that had LOMRs."

est_twfe = feols(log(sale_price) ~ i(rel_year_add_eff,  ref =c(-1, -1000 )) | 
                  pin + sale_year,   
                cluster = ~clean_name,
               # subset = ~in_lomr == "Not in LOMR" & !is.na(clean_name),
                data = df_prep)
#summary(est_twfe)
#iplot(est_twfe)

est_sa = feols(log(sale_price) ~ sunab(treat_year_add_eff, rel_year_add_eff) | pin + sale_year, 
                          cluster = ~clean_name,

  data = df_prep)

#summary(est_sa)

#iplot(mod1)
iplot(list(est_twfe, est_sa), main = "Mapped into Effective SFHA")


```


```{r}
#| label: fig-feolscomparison-prelimfirms
#| fig-cap: "Added to SFHA. Information shock = preliminary FIRM."


est_twfe = feols(log(sale_price) ~ i(rel_year_add_prelim,  ref =c(-1, -1000 )) | 
                  pin + sale_year, 
                cluster = ~clean_name, 
                data = df_prep)
#summary(est_did)
# iplot(est_did)

est_sa = feols(log(sale_price) ~ sunab(cohort = treat_year_add_prelim, period = rel_year_add_prelim, ref.p = c(-1,-1000)) | 
               pin + sale_year,
             cluster= ~clean_name,
             data = df_prep)

# modelsummary::modelsummary(mod1,
#                output = "flextable",
#                fmt = function(x) round(x, 2),
#              stars = TRUE)

iplot(list(est_twfe, est_sa), main = "Mapped into Prelim FIRMs")
```


```{r}
est_did2 = feols(log(sale_price) ~ i(rel_year_add_prelim,  ref =c(-1, -1000 )) | 
                  pin + sale_year,   
                cluster = ~clean_name,
                subset = ~in_lomr == "Not in LOMR" & !is.na(clean_name),
                data = df_prep)
#summary(est_did)

iplot(est_did2, main = "Mapped into Prelim. Excludes LOMR PINs.")
```



```{r}
#| label: fig-changereferenceyear
#| fig-cap: "TWFE. Changes reference year from -1 to -2. Includes in_lomr dummy."
#| eval: false
#| include: false


est_did1 = feols(log(sale_price) ~ in_lomr+i(rel_year_add_prelim,  ref =c(-1, -1000 )) | 
pin + sale_year, cluster=~clean_name, data = df_prep)


est_did2 = feols(log(sale_price) ~ in_lomr + i(rel_year_add_prelim,  ref =c(-2, -1000 )) | 
pin + sale_year, cluster=~clean_name, data = df_prep)

iplot(list(est_did1, est_did2), main = "Reference year as -1 and -2, Added to Prelim")

```






```{r}
#| label: fig-changereferenceyear2
#| fig-cap: "SA20. Changes reference year from -1 to -2."

mod1 = feols(log(sale_price) ~ sunab(treat_year_add_eff, rel_year_add_eff) | pin + sale_year, 
                          cluster = ~clean_name,

  data = df_prep)

summary(mod1)
mod2 = feols(log(sale_price) ~ sunab(cohort = treat_year_add_eff, period = rel_year_add_eff, ref.p = c(-2,-1000)) | pin + sale_year, 
             cluster = ~clean_name,
  data = df_prep)

summary(mod2)
#iplot(mod1)
iplot(list(mod1, mod2), main = "Reference year as -1 and -2, Added to Eff.")

```


```{r}
#| label: fig-removeprelims
#| fig-cap: "Removed from Prelim"

est_sa = feols(log(sale_price) ~ sunab(cohort = treat_year_remove_prelim, period = rel_year_remove_prelim) | pin + sale_year,
             cluster= ~clean_name,
             data = df_prep)
# modelsummary::modelsummary(est_sa,
#                            output = "flextable",
#                            fmt = function(x) round(x, 2),
#                            stars = TRUE)
# iplot(est_sa)


est_twfe = feols(log(sale_price) ~ i(rel_year_remove_prelim,  ref =c(-1, -1000 )) | 
                  pin + sale_year, cluster = ~clean_name, data = df_prep)
#summary(est_did)
#iplot(est_did)

# 
# prelim_noLOMRPINs = feols(log(sale_price) ~ i(rel_year_remove_prelim,  ref =c(-1, -1000 )) | 
#                             pin + sale_year,   
#                           cluster = ~clean_name,
#                           subset = ~in_lomr == "Not in LOMR" & !is.na(clean_name),
#                           data = df_prep)


iplot(list(est_sa, est_twfe), main = "Removed from Prelim")
```


### Callaway and Sant'Anna

```{r}
#| eval: false

library(did)

df_prep_cs <- df_prep |> 
  mutate(log_price = log(sale_price),
         pin_num = as.numeric(pin),
         treat_year_remove = ifelse(is.na(treat_year_remove), 0 , treat_year_remove)) |>
  filter(change_type != "Always SFHA")

attgt <- att_gt(yname = "log_price",
                        tname = "sale_year",
                        idname = "pin_num",
                        gname = "treat_year_remove",
                        data = df_prep_cs,
                allow_unbalanced_panel = TRUE
                        )
  
summary(attgt) 
saveRDS(attgt, file = "outputs/attgt_Q1_didoutput.rds")

did_graph <- ggdid(attgt)
did_graph

aggte_output_simp <- aggte(attgt, type = "simple", na.rm=TRUE)
summary(aggte_output_simp)

aggte_output_dynamic <- aggte(attgt, type = "dynamic", na.rm = TRUE)
summary(aggte_output_dynamic)
ggdid(aggte_output_dynamic)

agg_group <- aggte(attgt, type = "group", na.rm=TRUE)
ggdid(agg_group)
```

![](images/clipboard-2471470311.png)

![![](images/clipboard-728971117.png)](images/clipboard-2591789333.png)


## Effect of Insurance Requirement

- Reminder: Cohort should be fixed for each unit and represent the period when the treatment was received.   
- to get the canonical two-way fixed effects (TWFE) estimates, use interaction command  `i(factor_variable, number variable, reference_period)`    

- `iplot` reports coefficients of variables created with `i()` and only those variables. `coefplot()` would display all coefficients.   

If a PIN is in an SFHA and a LOMR, then the insurance requirement is removed. If measuring capitalization of the flood insurance cost, then whether or not flood insurance would be required for a federally backed mortgage is the key variable. This is what capitalization literature does. 


```{r}
lomr_pins <- res_sales |> filter(lomr_date != 0 ) |> select(pin, lomr_date, sfha2024, sfha2018) |> distinct()

table(lomr_pins$lomr_date)
table(df_prep$removedfrom_eff_sfha)
table(df_prep$in_eff_sfha)
table(df_prep$in_prelim_sfha)
```

```{r}

table(df_prep$sale_year)

df_prep <- df_prep |>
  left_join(lomr_pins) |>
  group_by(pin) |>
  arrange(sale_year) |>
  mutate(
    removed = if_else(dplyr::lag(ins_req == TRUE) & ins_req == FALSE, TRUE, FALSE),
    removed_ever = any(removed, na.rm=TRUE) )|>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
    removed_date = if_else(removed_ever==TRUE & lomr_date > eff_date, lomr_date, NA_Date_),
    removed_date = if_else(removed_ever==TRUE & is.na(removed_date), eff_date, removed_date,
        
      #  removed_year = year(remove_date)
 #mutate(
       #  removed_FIRM = ifelse(removed==TRUE  & in_lomr != "Received LOMR", TRUE, FALSE),

       #  removed_LOMR = ifelse(removed==TRUE & in_lomr == "Received LOMR", TRUE, FALSE),

  #) |>
#  removed_year = ifelse(any(removed==TRUE & (lomr_date > eff_date)), lomr_date, eff_date),
          #  max(eff_date[removed==TRUE], lomr_date[removed==TRUE]),
          # removed_FIRM_date = last(eff_date[removed_FIRM == TRUE]),
          # removed_LOMR_year = min(year(lomr_date[removed_LOMR == TRUE]))
  )) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(
  removed_year = if_else(!is.na(removed_date), year(removed_date), NA),
  rel_year_removed = if_else(!is.na(removed_year), as.integer(sale_year - removed_year), -1000))

     #    rel_year_FIRM_removed = round(lubridate::as.difftime(sale_date - removed_FIRM_date) / 365),
     #     rel_year_FIRM_removed = round(lubridate::as.difftime(sale_date - removed_FIRM_date) / 365)
    # ) #|>
 # mutate(across(rel_year_removed, is.infinite, -1000))


# df_prep[duplicated(df_prep),] # 5440 duplicates? from years 2010 to 2014 only.
table(df_prep$lomr_date)

df_prep <- df_prep |> distinct(pin, sale_date, sale_price, .keep_all = TRUE)
```


```{r}
df_prep$removed_year[is.na(df_prep$removed_year)] <- 10000
#df_prep$rel_year_removed[is.na(df_prep$rel_year_removed)] <- -1000
#df_prep$rel_year_removed[is.infinite(df_prep$rel_year_removed)] <- -1000

table(df_prep$removed_date)
table(df_prep$removed_ever)

table(df_prep$removed_year)
table(df_prep$rel_year_removed)

#df_prep$removed_year = as.factor(df_prep$removed_year)

```

```{r}
twfe_removed2 = feols(log(sale_price) ~ removed  | 
                      pin + sale_year,   
                    cluster = ~clean_name,
                    data = df_prep#,
                  #  subset = ~ rel_year_removed!= -12
)
twfe_removed2|> etable()

```

```{r}

mod2 <- feols(log(sale_price) ~ sunab(cohort = removed_year, period = rel_year_removed#,
                                      #ref.c = "10000", ref.p = c(-1, -1000)
                                      ) | pin + sale_year,
              cluster = ~clean_name,
              data = df_prep              )
#etable(mod2)
#summary(mod2, agg= "att")

twfe_removed= feols(log(sale_price) ~ i(rel_year_removed,  ref =c(-1, -1000 ))  | 
                      pin + sale_year,   
                    cluster = ~clean_name,
                    data = df_prep#,
                             #     subset = ~ rel_year_removed != -12
)

#summary(twfe_removed)
#iplot(twfe_removed)


iplot(list(twfe_removed, mod2), #xlim = c(-8, 8), 
      main = "Effect of Removal from SFHA (LOMR or FIRM Update)")
legend("topleft", col = c(1, 2), pch = c(20, 15), 
       legend = c("TWFE", "S&A (2020)"))

# switches order color/icon order
# iplot(list(mod2, twfe_removed), 
#       main = "Effect of Removal from SFHA (LOMR or FIRM Update)")
# legend("topleft", col = c(1, 2), pch = c(20, 15), 
#        legend = c("S&A (2020)", "TWFE"))
```


```{r}
#| label: excludeslomrs

mod2 <- feols(log(sale_price) ~ sunab(cohort = removed_year, period = rel_year_removed, 
                                      ref.p = c(-1, -1000))  | pin + sale_year,
              cluster= ~clean_name,
              data = (df_prep |> filter(in_lomr == "Not in LOMR"))
              )
#etable(mod2)
#summary(mod2, agg= "att")
# iplot(mod2)

#table(df_prep$in_lomr)
twfe_removed <-  feols(log(sale_price) ~ i(rel_year_removed,
                                           ref =c(-1, -1000 ))  | 
                         pin + sale_year,   
                       cluster = ~clean_name,
              data = (df_prep |> filter(in_lomr == "Not in LOMR"))
                     #  subset = ~in_lomr == "Not in LOMR"
)

#etable(twfe_removed)
#summary(twfe_removed, agg= "att")
#iplot(twfe_removed)

iplot(list(mod2, twfe_removed), 
      main = "Effect of Removal from SFHA (No LOMRs PINs Included)")
legend("topleft", col = c(1, 2), pch = c(20, 17), 
       legend = c("S&A (2020)", "TWFE"))


```



# Transaction trends

## Volume

```{r}
res_sales |>
  group_by(sale_year, change_type, eff_date) |> 
  summarize(n_txn = n(), .groups = "drop") |>  # number of transactions
  ggplot(aes(x = sale_year, y = n_txn, color = change_type)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    x = "Year of Sale",
    y = "Number of Transactions",
    color = "Property Type",
    title = "Number of Transactions by SFHA & Flood Factor Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top") + 
  facet_wrap(~eff_date)



res_sales |>
  group_by(sale_year, change_type, eff_date) |> 
  summarize(n_txn = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  mutate(indexed = n_txn / n_txn[sale_year==2012], .by = c(change_type, eff_date)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sale_year, y = indexed, color = change_type)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    x = "Year of Sale",
    y = "Transactions indexed to 2012 volume",
    color = "Property Type",
    title = "Number of Transactions by SFHA & Flood Factor Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top") + 
  facet_wrap(~eff_date) +
  scale_y_continuous(label =scales::percent)


```

```{r}
res_sales |>
  mutate(
    ff_label = ifelse(high_ff_score, "High FF", "Low FF"),
    sfha_label = ifelse(in_eff_sfha == "SFHA", "In SFHA", "Not in SFHA"),
    group = paste(ff_label, sfha_label, sep = " & ")
  ) |>
  group_by(sale_year, group) |>   # replace sale_year with your actual year variable
  summarize(n_txn = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sale_year, y = n_txn, color = group)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    x = "Year of Sale",
    y = "Number of Transactions",
    color = "Property Type",
    title = "Number of Transactions by SFHA & Flood Factor Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

```

## Indexed Volume of Sales

```{r}
res_sales |>
  mutate(
    ff_label = ifelse(high_ff_score, "High FF", "Low FF"),
    sfha_label = ifelse(in_eff_sfha == "SFHA", "In SFHA", "Not in SFHA"),
    group = paste(ff_label, sfha_label, sep = " & ")
  ) |>
  group_by(sale_year, group) |>
  summarize(n_txn = n(), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(group) |>
  mutate(
    base2012 = n_txn[sale_year == 2012],
    index2012 = n_txn / base2012
  ) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sale_year, y = index2012, color = group)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Year of Sale",
    y = "Indexed Transactions (2012 = 100%)",
    color = "Property Type",
    title = "Relative Change in Transactions by SFHA & Flood Factor Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

```

```{r}

# 1) Build a quarterly decimal from sale_date
res_q <- res_sales |>
  mutate(
    # ensure Date
    sale_date = as.Date(sale_date),
    year  = year(sale_date),
    qtr   = quarter(sale_date),                 # 1..4
    sale_qtr_dec = year + (qtr - 1) / 4,        # 2012.00, .25, .50, .75
    ff_label  = ifelse(high_ff_score, "High FF", "Low FF"),
    sfha_label = ifelse(in_eff_sfha == "SFHA", "In SFHA", "Not in SFHA"),
    group = paste(ff_label, sfha_label, sep = " & "),
    time_year = sale_year - 2010
  ) |>
  group_by(sale_qtr_dec, group) |>
  summarise(n_txn = n(), .groups = "drop")

# (Optional) Fill missing quarters with 0 so lines don’t break
res_q <- res_q |>
  group_by(group) |>
  complete(sale_qtr_dec = seq(floor(min(sale_qtr_dec, na.rm = TRUE)),
                              ceiling(max(sale_qtr_dec, na.rm = TRUE)) - 0.75,
                              by = 0.25),
           fill = list(n_txn = 0)) |>
  ungroup()

# 2A) Plot raw quarterly counts
p_counts <- res_q |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sale_qtr_dec, y = n_txn, color = group)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 1.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(floor(min(res_q$sale_qtr_dec, na.rm = TRUE)),
                 ceiling(max(res_q$sale_qtr_dec, na.rm = TRUE)),
                 by = 1),
    labels = function(x) sprintf("%d", x)  # show clean years on ticks
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Sale Quarter (decimal years)",
    y = "Number of Transactions",
    color = "Property Type",
    title = "Quarterly Transactions by SFHA & Flood Factor Status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

p_counts

# Choose base quarter explicitly: 2012 Q1 (i.e., 2012.00)
res_q_idx <- res_q |>
  group_by(group) |>
  mutate(
    base_val = n_txn[sale_qtr_dec == 2012.00],
    index_2012q1 = ifelse(is.na(base_val) | base_val == 0, NA_real_, n_txn / base_val)
  ) |>
  ungroup()

p_index <- res_q_idx |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sale_qtr_dec, y = index_2012q1, color = group)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 1.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = seq(floor(min(res_q_idx$sale_qtr_dec, na.rm = TRUE)),
                 ceiling(max(res_q_idx$sale_qtr_dec, na.rm = TRUE)),
                 by = 1),
    labels = function(x) sprintf("%d", x)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Sale Quarter (decimal years)",
    y = "Indexed Transactions (2012 Q1 = 100%)",
    color = "Property Type",
    title = "Relative Quarterly Change by SFHA & Flood Factor (2012 Q1 = 100%)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

p_index

```

```{r}
res_q_idx_season <- res_q |>
  mutate(
    year = floor(sale_qtr_dec),                          # extract year
    qtr  = 1 + (sale_qtr_dec - year) * 4                 # recover quarter (1–4)
  ) |>
  group_by(group, qtr) |>
  mutate(
    base_val = n_txn[year == 2012],                      # use 2012's value for that quarter
    rel_index = ifelse(is.na(base_val) | base_val == 0,
                       NA_real_,
                       n_txn / base_val)
  ) |>
  ungroup()

ggplot(res_q_idx_season, aes(x = sale_qtr_dec, y = rel_index, color = group)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 1.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Sale Quarter (decimal years)",
    y = "Indexed Transactions (2012 Quarter = 100%)",
    color = "Property Type",
    title = "Relative Quarterly Change (Seasonally Indexed to 2012)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")
```

## Transaction volume for properties outside of SFHAs

```{r}
#| label: fig-floodfactorcomparison
#| fig-cap: "Figure for Question 2. Will move it to correct file eventually."

ggplot(res_q_idx_season |> filter(group %in% c("High FF & Not in SFHA","Low FF & Not in SFHA")), aes(x = sale_qtr_dec, y = rel_index, color = group)) +
  geom_line(size = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 1.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Sale Quarter (decimal years)",
    y = "Indexed Transactions (2012 Quarter = 100%)",
    color = "Property Type",
    title = "Relative Quarterly Change (Seasonally Indexed to 2012)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top")

```

```{r}
#| label: fig-FIRMupdates
#| fig-cap: "Indexed transaction volume for properties outside of SFHA. Shown by geographic areas (based on FIRM effective dates). Uses df_prep which has PINs that sold 2+ times instead of all residentialsales."

res_sales |>
  mutate(
    ff_label = ifelse(high_ff_score, "High FF", "Low FF"),
    sfha_label = ifelse(in_eff_sfha == "SFHA", "In SFHA", "Not in SFHA"),
    group = paste(ff_label, sfha_label, sep = " & ")
  ) |>
    filter(group %in% c("High FF & Not in SFHA","Low FF & Not in SFHA")) |>

  group_by(group, eff_date, sale_year) |>
  summarize(n_txn = n()) |>
  ungroup() |>
  group_by(group, eff_date) |>
  mutate(
    base2012 = n_txn[sale_year == 2012],
    index2012 = n_txn / base2012) |>
  ungroup() |>
  ggplot(aes(x = sale_year, y = index2012, color = group), group = eff_date) +
  geom_line(size = 0.9) +
  geom_point(size = 1.6) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Sale Year",
    y = "Indexed Transactions (2012  = 100%)",
    color = "Property Type",
    title = "Relative Yearly Change (Seasonally Indexed to 2012)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "top") +
  facet_wrap(~eff_date)

```
